body: {"detail":"ambiguous_scope: The prompt is not safely rewritable without choosing between conflicting scopes. It mixes stale tracker-lock reclamation (`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:216-239`), Land behavior (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:963-1045` and `:1382-1430`), and preclaim 'local-ahead' terminology (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_preclaim_test.go:132-150`). The repo already has generic stale-lock coverage in `TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery` (`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go:389-424`), so the missing gap is not uniquely pinned down. Rewriting would require deciding whether the bead is about lock recovery, post-recovery Land semantics, or preclaim state, which changes scope.","intake_outcome":"operator_required"}

{"rationale":"Bead has solid structure (clear PROBLEM/CURRENT STATE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE, file:line refs, numbered AC naming a Test function, deps, proper labels, lefthook/go test gates). However, it carries an unresolved needs-human flag for ambiguous_scope: title still references 'PreClaimFetch race' while body asserts network I/O has been removed (stale framing); reviewer noted existing TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery at tracker_lock_test.go:389-424 already covers generic stale-lock recovery, so the uniquely-missing gap is not crisply pinned down; and the AC does not differentiate the new test from existing coverage (e.g., asserting local-ahead state survives recovery without re-merge). A sub-agent given only this body would reasonably hesitate between duplicating existing coverage and inventing scope.","score":5,"suggested_fixes":["Drop 'PreClaimFetch race' from the title — it contradicts the body's claim that network I/O is removed from Land().","Add an AC line that explicitly distinguishes the new test from TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery (tracker_lock_test.go:389-424), e.g., assert that after lock break the worker observes local main already advanced and does NOT re-merge or roll back.","Pin the simulated crash point to a specific code location (e.g., 'after UpdateRefTo at execute_bead_land.go:NNNN but before evidence commit') so the test setup is unambiguous.","Resolve or clear the needs-human-* custom fields before dispatch; an unresolved ambiguous_scope flag should block autonomous execution per P7."],"waivers_applied":[]}

status=open
reason=AC#5 blocked by pre-existing unrelated test breakage in cli/internal/agent/bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go — the checked-in skill mirrors under .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ and .claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ were intentionally removed (commits 42c333d61 / d530f68cd / c536d2ff1, "chore: remove checked-in DDx skill mirrors [ddx-11ef34e1]") but TestBeadLifecycleSkillReadinessDocumentsRewriteContract / TestBeadLifecycleSkillLintDocumentsRationaleShape / TestBeadLifecycleSkillFilesStayInSyncOnContractTerms were not updated and still try to os.Open those removed paths. Because lefthook's go-test step runs the entire internal/agent package, every commit that touches a file in that package now trips the stale tests — including this bead's targeted test addition. lefthook pre-commit thus fails before AC#5 can be observed. what is done: cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go gains TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand (synchronous, no wall-clock sleeps, t.TempDir() sandbox via the existing newLandTestRepo helper). It simulates worker 1 advancing local main via UpdateRefTo, leaves a lock dir owned by a known-dead PID (spawn-then-reap), and verifies worker 2's Land() breaks the stale lock, fast-forwards to its own ResultRev, preserves worker 1's commit on main (no rollback), and produces no merge commit. AC#1, #2, #3, and #4 are all satisfied — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' -count=1` is green (TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount PASS + TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand PASS). The full patch is preserved at .ddx/executions/20260514T164952-8fb7870e/tracker_lock_test.patch so the change is recoverable after the worktree is reaped. what blocks: lefthook -> go-test -> internal/agent suite runs TestBeadLifecycleSkill* which now FAIL on missing .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md (the source lives only at library/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md after ddx-11ef34e1's cleanup). Resolving requires either deleting the stale contract tests or pointing them at the surviving library path — both outside this bead's area:lock / kind:test scope. what a follow-up needs: file a sibling bead under area:agent / kind:test titled "delete or repoint stale skill-mirror contract tests" that removes (or updates the paths in) bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go to match the post-ddx-11ef34e1 layout, land it, then re-dispatch ddx-c019e48a — at which point the on-disk diff (or the saved patch) can be staged and committed cleanly. Retry is safe; the test itself does not depend on the broken contract tests.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

Because lefthook's go-test step runs the entire internal/agent package, every commit that touches a file in that package now trips the stale tests — including this bead's targeted test addition. lefthook pre-commit thus fails before AC#5 can be observed.

what is done: cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go gains TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand (synchronous, no wall-clock sleeps, t.TempDir() sandbox via the existing newLandTestRepo helper). It simulates worker 1 advancing local main via UpdateRefTo, leaves a lock dir owned by a known-dead PID (spawn-then-reap), and verifies worker 2's Land() breaks the stale lock, fast-forwards to its own ResultRev, preserves worker 1's commit on main (no rollback), and produces no merge commit. AC#1, #2, #3, and #4 are all satisfied — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerLock_' -count=1` is green (TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount PASS + TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand PASS). The full patch is preserved at .ddx/executions/20260514T164952-8fb7870e/tracker_lock_test.patch so the change is recoverable after the worktree is reaped.

what blocks: lefthook -> go-test -> internal/agent suite runs TestBeadLifecycleSkill* which now FAIL on missing .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md (the source lives only at library/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md after ddx-11ef34e1's cleanup). Resolving requires either deleting the stale contract tests or pointing them at the surviving library path — both outside this bead's area:lock / kind:test scope.

what a follow-up needs: file a sibling bead under area:agent / kind:test titled "delete or repoint stale skill-mirror contract tests" that removes (or updates the paths in) bead_lifecycle_skill_contract_test.go to match the post-ddx-11ef34e1 layout, land it, then re-dispatch ddx-c019e48a — at which point the on-disk diff (or the saved patch) can be staged and committed cleanly. Retry is safe; the test itself does not depend on the broken contract tests.
